behavior.business_purpose: The Codemod Factory lets the single local operator state a repo-wide structural refactor in plain English, have an LLM compile it into a precise ts-morph AST transform, see a per-file diff across the whole project as a dry-run, then apply only the chosen files behind a hard write-confinement boundary. It is the board's only mechanism for human-gated, project-wide mechanical edits that bypass the per-ticket agent/worktree pipeline. The design's whole point is a trustworthy preview->apply gate over a destructive, blast-radius operation (rewrite every TS file) with the safety interlocks visible.

actors.name: operator
actors.notes: single local user driving the CodemodPanel overlay (x key / command palette) or hitting the HTTP API directly

actors.name: llm
actors.notes: claude-cli invokeClaudePrompt compiles intent->ts-morph transform body; not an external caller, an internal compiler step

entry_points.observable: compiles intent->transform (or runs a supplied script), dry-runs over all TS files, returns {script, files:[{filePath,relativePath,diff,original,modified}], totalTsFiles, limitReached}; writes nothing
writes the supplied modified content for selected in-repo files; returns {applied[], skipped[]}; rejects any path outside repoPath
lists saved codemods (agent_skills filtered to type==='codemod') for project + global
saves {name,description,script} as an agent_skills row patched to type='codemod'; 201, or 409 on name collision in scope
full describe->preview->select-files->apply->save workflow; stats bar, collapsible generated script, colorized per-file diff checkbox list

behaviors.statement: Previewing an intent compiles it into a ts-morph transform and returns dry-run per-file diffs across the whole project without writing anything
preconditions: project registered with a resolvable repoPath
LLM (claude-cli) reachable, OR a pre-existing script supplied to skip generation
behaviors.observable_outcome: 200 with a generated script string, a files[] of changed files each carrying unified diff + original/modified, totalTsFiles count, limitReached flag; the project's files on disk are unchanged

behaviors.statement: Preview refuses without the two required inputs (description and projectId), returning a 400 that names the missing field
behaviors.observable_outcome: 400 with error matching /description/i when description absent; 400 with error matching /projectId/i when projectId absent

behaviors.statement: A project with more than 100 TS files refuses to preview until the caller explicitly acknowledges the blast radius via overrideLimit:true
behaviors.observable_outcome: ValidationError surfaced instructing the caller to resend overrideLimit:true (the UI shows a yellow 'run on all files' override); resending with overrideLimit:true proceeds to diff

behaviors.statement: Apply confines every write to the project repo: a target path that resolves outside repoPath is rejected and the file on disk is left untouched (the core security boundary)
preconditions: a change whose filePath is outside repoPath (foreign absolute or .. traversal)
behaviors.observable_outcome: 400 with error matching /outside the project/i; the out-of-repo file's content is unchanged

behaviors.statement: Apply writes the supplied modified content to disk for the selected in-repo files (empty selection = all changed files) and reports applied vs skipped
behaviors.observable_outcome: 200 with {applied[], skipped[]}; the named files now contain the modified text; applied contains the written path and skipped is empty when all selected

behaviors.statement: Apply refuses without projectId (the security boundary) or with an empty changes array, returning 400
behaviors.observable_outcome: 400 with error matching /projectId/i when projectId absent; 400 when changes is missing/empty

behaviors.statement: A working transform is saved as a named reusable codemod (agent_skills row tagged type='codemod') and appears in the codemod listing; required fields are enforced and a name collision in scope is refused
behaviors.observable_outcome: 201 with {id, name, type:'codemod'}; the row appears in GET /api/codemods with type==='codemod'; 400 when name or script missing; 409 when an existing type='codemod' name collides in scope

behaviors.statement: The codemod listing returns only agent_skills discriminated as type='codemod', never plain skills
behaviors.observable_outcome: 200 array where every item has type==='codemod'

behaviors.statement: Fetching a saved codemod by id returns it, or 404 for an unknown id
behaviors.observable_outcome: 200 with the codemod when the id exists; 404 with error when it does not

permissions.notes: single-user local app; no RBAC. The only meaningful 'permission' is the repoPath write-confinement boundary, modelled as codemods.apply.confine-writes, not a role check.

error_states.statement: Invalid LLM-emitted transform code fails loudly at compile time as a ValidationError (new AsyncFunction wrap), not mid-apply
Apply target outside repoPath -> ValidationError 'Refusing to write outside the project' (400)
>100 TS files without overrideLimit -> ValidationError requesting confirmation
Saving a codemod whose name already exists as type='codemod' in scope -> 409

external_integrations: ts-morph (AST engine; LLM code calls its API directly)
agent_skills table + agent-skill.repository (saved-codemod persistence, overloaded via type discriminator)

unknowns.question: Does the limit guard (>100 TS files) ever actually fire for the real E2E project, and is overrideLimit honored end-to-end?
unknowns.why_unknown: the E2E project is small; not exercised live, no test triggers the >100 path

unknowns.question: Does preview produce a real, correct ts-morph transform and accurate diff for a non-trivial refactor?
unknowns.why_unknown: the only preview test is a deliberate no-op and tolerates a 500 (AI unavailable in CI); transform correctness and diff fidelity are never asserted

unknowns.question: Is the saved-codemod re-run path (preview with a supplied script) reachable from the UI?
unknowns.why_unknown: panel saves but exposes no visible list/pick/re-run control; capability exists at the API only (domain doc Risks)

unknowns.question: Is the 500-line per-file diff cap (preview truncated, apply writes full content) observable/asserted anywhere?
unknowns.why_unknown: no test exercises a >500-line file; cosmetic/trust gap flagged in domain doc, unverified

covered_by.test: packages/e2e/tests/api/codemod.test.ts::POST /api/codemods/preview with valid project returns script and file list

behaviors.note: Closed by e2e-test-author wave-2 2026-06-27 (adversarially reviewed). codemod-preview-generate.test.ts

gap.rationale: The >100-TS-file blast-radius guard and its overrideLimit override path are never exercised. This is the module's scale safety interlock and has no test at either the block or the override-and-proceed edge.

covered_by.test: packages/e2e/tests/api/codemod.test.ts::POST /api/codemods/apply refuses to write outside the project

history.last_regression: fix(codemods) path-traversal/arbitrary-write hole (domain doc Security invariant)

gap.rationale: The write happy-path is fully asserted (file content verified on disk, applied/skipped reported). But the 'skips files not in selectedFiles' test passes an EMPTY selectedFiles (which means apply-all) and only asserts applied+skipped==1 -- it never actually verifies that a NON-selected file is skipped/left unwritten. The selective-skip semantic is touched, not asserted.

Create (201, type='codemod', appears in list) and required-field 400s are asserted. The 409 name-collision branch (routes/codemods.ts:120-125) -- the uniqueness invariant that makes 'the X codemod' addressable -- is never exercised.

Only the 404-unknown-id branch is asserted. The success path (GET /:id returning an existing saved codemod's body) is never directly fetched/asserted -- the save test verifies presence via the list endpoint, not via GET /:id.

top_gaps.rationale: Scale safety interlock with zero coverage at both the block and override-and-proceed edges. Lower ROI because triggering >100 files needs a large fixture tree (higher exec/maint cost) and the blast-radius failure is a UX annoyance, not data loss. impact 3 x regression 3 / (exec 3 + maint 2.5) = ~1.6, lifted into P3 by being a named invariant with no test. Could be unit-tested against CODEMOD_FILE_LIMIT cheaply if the limit were injectable.

The 409 name-collision branch (uniqueness invariant for reusable codemods) is unasserted. Cheap to add: POST the same name twice, assert second is 409. impact 2 x regression 2 / (exec 1 + maint 1) = 2.0.
